Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class BotVersion

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.BotVersion
public class BotVersion extends Metadata

Java class for BotVersion complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="BotVersion">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="articleAnswersGPTEnabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botDialogGroups" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotDialogGroup" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botDialogs" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotDialog" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="citationsEnabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="conversationGoals" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationDefinitionGoal" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="conversationSystemDialogs" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationSystemDialog" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="conversationVariables" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationVariable" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryDialog" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="intentDisambiguationEnabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="intentV3Enabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="knowledgeFallbackEnabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="mainMenuDialog" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="nlpProviders" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationDefinitionNlpProvider" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="responseDelayMilliseconds" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="smallTalkEnabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="status" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotStatusCode"/>
         <element name="version" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • isArticleAnswersGPTEnabled

      public Boolean isArticleAnswersGPTEnabled()
      Gets the value of the articleAnswersGPTEnabled property.
      Returns:
      possible object is Boolean

    • setArticleAnswersGPTEnabled

      public void setArticleAnswersGPTEnabled(Boolean value)
      Sets the value of the articleAnswersGPTEnabled property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is Boolean

    • getBotDialogGroups

      public List<BotDialogGroup> getBotDialogGroups()
      Gets the value of the botDialogGroups property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the botDialogGroups property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBotDialogGroups().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotDialogGroup

      Returns:
      The value of the botDialogGroups property.

    • getBotDialogs

      public List<BotDialog> getBotDialogs()
      Gets the value of the botDialogs property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the botDialogs property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBotDialogs().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotDialog

      Returns:
      The value of the botDialogs property.

      public List<ConversationDefinitionGoal> getConversationGoals()
      Gets the value of the conversationGoals property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the conversationGoals property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getConversationGoals().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ConversationDefinitionGoal

      Returns:
      The value of the conversationGoals property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the conversationSystemDialogs property.

       getConversationVariables().add(newItem);

    • getNlpProviders

      public List<ConversationDefinitionNlpProvider> getNlpProviders()
      Gets the value of the nlpProviders property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the nlpProviders property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getNlpProviders().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ConversationDefinitionNlpProvider

      Returns:
      The value of the nlpProviders property.
